question_html:
C and Si both have the same lattice structure, having 4 bonding electrons in each. However, C is an insulator whereas Si is an intrinsic semiconductor. This is because

  1. In case of C the valence band is not completely filled at absolute zero temperature.
  2. In case of C the conduction band is partly filled even at absolute zero temperature.
  3. The four bonding electrons in the case of C lie in the second orbit, whereas in the case of Si they lie in the third.
  4. The four bonding electrons in the case of C lie in the third orbit, whereas for Si they lie in the fourth orbit.
question_text: C and Si both have the same lattice structure, having 4 bonding electrons in each. However, C is an insulator whereas Si is an intrinsic semiconductor. This is because In case of C the valence band is not completely filled at absolute zero temperature. In case of C the conduction band is partly filled even at absolute zero temperature. The four bonding electrons in the case of C lie in the second orbit, whereas in the case of Si they lie in the third. The four bonding electrons in the case of C lie in the third orbit, whereas for Si they lie in the fourth orbit.
